summary.summary: Reworks Core Data usage and reduces N+1 queries across storages: long‑lived shared NSManagedObjectContext instances were replaced with per‑operation task contexts created via CoreDataStack.shared.newTaskContext(), many storage classes (PumpHistoryStorage, CarbsStorage, GlucoseStorage, DeterminationStorage, OverrideStorage, TempTargetsStorage, etc.) were adapted to use a contextProvider/makeContext pattern, and CoreDataStack background-context behavior was adjusted (removal of automaticallyMergesChangesFromParent). Several fetches were changed to prefetch relationships (bolus/tempBasal on PumpEventStored, forecastValues on OrefDetermination) to eliminate N+1 faults. TDD weighted average was optimized to use SQL aggregate queries instead of full-row loads. The custom Core Data change publisher was replaced with a history-sourced entityChangePublisher and multiple places migrated to NSFetchedResultsController; pump-event dedup was changed to use a (timestamp,type) key plus a batched find-or-create with a uniqueness constraint backstop. Additional bugfixes and safety guards (e.g., an APSManager crash fix and various pipeline teardown / debounce / stale-run protections) were included.
summary.userFacingImpact: Intended outcomes: reduced Core Data contention and stale‑object races, fewer SQL round‑trips (lower CPU / I/O on hot paths), faster TDD aggregate calculations, and more robust cross‑process / batch change detection. A crash in APSManager due to incorrect context usage was explicitly fixed. The author reports ~2 months of personal testing but flags that the changes must be tested thoroughly; there is a risk that previously unused code paths could trigger ConcurrencyDebug crashes if any wrong NSManagedObjectContext access was accidentally introduced. Human review and broad testing are recommended before release.
